titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Marion Belanger, Rift/Fault
titlestmt.author: by Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Sunday March 28, 2021 at 03:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Title page and inscriptions in English.

did.abstract: Photographs created by Marion Belanger, 2006-2012 and printed 2015 that document tectonic shifting of the North American Plate in California and Iceland.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs created by Marion Belanger, 2006-2012 and printed 2015 that document tectonic shifting of the North American Plate in California and Iceland. Images in the "Rift" component document the rising eastern edge of the North American Continental Plate where it meets the Eurasian Plate along the Mid-Atlantic Rift in Iceland and include views of geothermal energy projects and the built environment. Images in the "Fault" component represents the subsiding western edge of the North American Plate along the San Andreas Fault in California and depict the built environment adjacent to the fault.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Radius Books on the Frederick W. and Carrie S. Beinecke Fund for Western Americana, 2016.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged under headings for Rift, Fault, and Ancillary Material.

bioghist.head: Marion Belanger (born 1957)
bioghist.p: Marion Belanger, Yale MFA 1990, is a photographer. Her work focuses on the cultural landscape, particularly where geology and the built environment intersect.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Marion Belanger, Rift/Fault. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit. These materials have been arranged and described according to national and local standards. For more information, please refer to the Beinecke Manuscript Unit Processing Manual.
Each folder in the collection contains a single inkjet print.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The Marion Belanger Photographs are the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Views of sites along edges of the North American Plate including the Mid-Atlantic Rift in Iceland and San Andreas Fault in California created in 2006 and printed in 2015-2016 as ink jet prints, 12.875 x 16.375 inches (32.7 x 41.6 centimeters), from an edition numbered one of five, and housed in Box 1, as well as 17 x 22 inches (43.2 x 55.9 cm) from an edition numbered one of four, and housed in Box 3. Ancillary material in Box 2 includes a box with a mounted inkjet print, a signed title page, and interleaving paper.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized under three headings for Rift, Fault, and Ancillary Material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inkjet print mounted on box cover depicts a composite of Rift # 11 and Fault #27
